_comment: Trigger-eval cases for skills/detect-and-analyze/SKILL.md. Each case has a prompt and a reason explaining the expected routing decision.

should_fire.prompt: How many boxes are on the conveyor belt right now?
should_fire.reason: Direct per-instance count question — object-level, classic detect-and-analyze trigger

should_fire.prompt: I need to count the number of people in each camera frame
should_fire.reason: Count people per frame — matches 'count people/vehicles/items in camera feeds'

should_fire.prompt: Detect and count cars in this parking lot image
should_fire.reason: Literal 'detect and count' phrasing — object-level count

should_fire.prompt: I want to tally the items on the assembly line automatically
should_fire.reason: Tally = per-instance count; assembly line = conveyor context

should_fire.prompt: Can you tell me the number of defects appearing in each product image?
should_fire.reason: Number of X per image — object-level count per instance, not an image-level verdict

should_fire.prompt: Count the defective items on the production line
should_fire.reason: Boundary case — 'count the defective items' asks for per-instance count (object-level) not image-level pass/fail — routes here not to classify-or-flag

should_fire.prompt: How wide are the boxes on the conveyor? Estimate size from the camera feed
should_fire.reason: Size estimation from bbox geometry — measurement-from-boxes capability of detect-and-analyze

should_fire.prompt: Extract a crop of each detected part from the inspection image
should_fire.reason: Per-object crop / ROI extraction — part of detect-and-analyze bbox output capabilities

should_fire.prompt: Give me an object tally from warehouse camera images — boxes and pallets
should_fire.reason: Object tally phrasing + multi-class — per-instance count intent

should_fire.prompt: Build me a model that counts trucks entering the facility from RTSP footage
should_fire.reason: Count trucks per frame — object-level; line-cross counting is track-and-count, but per-frame count is detect-and-analyze

should_not_fire.prompt: Is this weld defective or not? Flag bad parts.
should_not_fire.reason: Image-level verdict (defective yes/no for the whole image) — routes to classify-or-flag (M4), not detect-and-analyze

should_not_fire.prompt: Flag this whole product image as defective or pass
should_not_fire.reason: Image-level pass/fail classification — classify-or-flag (M4); no per-instance count requested

should_not_fire.prompt: Track the forklift as it moves through the warehouse and alert when it enters Zone B
should_not_fire.reason: Identity-linked tracking across frames + zone-entry — routes to track-and-count (M4)

should_not_fire.prompt: Measure the exact area of corrosion in millimeters squared from the image
should_not_fire.reason: Pixel-precise area requires masks, not bboxes — routes to segment-and-analyze (M4)

should_not_fire.prompt: Read the serial number and expiry date from the product label
should_not_fire.reason: Text extraction / OCR — routes to read-text (M4)

should_not_fire.prompt: How much does Roboflow's managed deployment cost for 5 streams running 24/7?
should_not_fire.reason: Cost/scale question with no unsolved detection problem — routes to economics-consultant

should_not_fire.prompt: I already have a trained YOLOv8 model. How do I export it to TensorRT?
should_not_fire.reason: User already has a working model and asks only about export — out of scope

should_not_fire.prompt: Compare self-hosting vs managed Roboflow for my 10-stream production setup
Help me set up active learning on my existing defect detection model
should_not_fire.reason: Active learning on an existing model — not a new detection build problem
